topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of old paper and faint lavender, usually a comforting backdrop to my shared room, suddenly felt suffocating. I walked in just as Liam was settling onto his bed, a book resting open on his chest. My eyes snagged on the page—my journal. My breath hitched, a shallow, rapid thing in my chest. I froze mid-step, my hand hovering near the doorknob. He didn't look up immediately, just turned a page with a deliberate, slow motion. The silence stretched, thick and heavy, pressing down on my eardrums. A coldness began to creep up my spine, a clammy sensation that made my palms instantly slick. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the hallway, but my feet felt rooted to the worn carpet. When he finally glanced over, his expression was unreadable, a strange mixture of nonchalance and something else—a knowing shadow. My throat tightened, making it difficult to draw a full breath.

[story 2]

It was the way the light hit the desk, illuminating the open pages of my diary, that first alerted me. I’d left it open on my nightstand, a careless oversight. When I finally turned to retrieve it, the ink seemed to swim under my gaze. Liam was sitting at his desk across the room, ostensibly studying, but his attention was fixed entirely on the book. I watched his jaw clench almost imperceptibly. A tremor started in my hands; I gripped the edge of my textbook so tightly my knuckles turned white. Every small sound—the hum of the refrigerator, the distant traffic—seemed amplified, sharp intrusions in the quiet. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my mouth alarming. When he finally looked up, his eyes met mine for a fleeting second, and in that glance, I saw something intense, something that made the air evacuate my lungs entirely. I quickly slammed the journal shut, the sound echoing loudly in the small space, and retreated to the safety of my own side of the room.

[story 3]

I found the evidence tucked beneath my pillow. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat I couldn't control. It was my journal, lying open to a page detailing a private anxiety, and Liam was sitting nearby, reading it. The reality of the situation crashed over me, leaving me momentarily paralyzed. I could feel a sudden, intense pressure building behind my eyes, a dizzying sensation. I took a shaky step toward him, my voice coming out as a strained whisper. "Liam, what are you doing?" His head snapped up, and the casual demeanor vanished, replaced by a look of startled surprise. He closed the book slowly, deliberately, and the sudden shift in atmosphere made me want to shrink into the mattress. I could only stare, my gaze darting between the closed book and his suddenly wide eyes, waiting for whatever came next.
